title: Dark Stone Magazine #1 - Flip Through / An Ode to the Humble Magazine (Shadows of Brimstone)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Sl5Fl_vOthI

Daniel (@dungeondive) opens the inaugural issue of Darkstone Magazine and embarks on a love letter to the physical magazine itself. What makes magazines special in an age of infinite digital scroll? For Daniel, it's the finite nature of the form—the deliberate editorial choices, the commitment to space-constrained layouts, the care taken with typography and photography. Unlike endless blogs and social feeds, magazines have structure: a beginning, middle, and end. Each monthly issue becomes a collectible artifact that rewards repeated reading, revealing new details with each pass.

Darkstone Magazine's first issue lives up to the format's promise, packed with themed threat deck recipes, painting tutorials, battle reports from Dicefest, and gorgeous art previews of upcoming Shadows of Brimstone expansions. The magazine reads like White Dwarf's more focused cousin—dedicated entirely to Flying Frog's dungeon-crawling world. Daniel finds himself once again hungry for more Shadows of Brimstone content, while subtly lobbying for similar treatment of Fortune and Glory and A Touch of Evil. At $14.99 per issue, it's a premium product unburdened by algorithm-driven rage bait, but absolutely worth the investment for those who crave curated, beautifully designed gaming material.

----

What magazines do you currently subscribe to, either digitally or in print? And would you back a quarterly magazine focused on other board game universes?
